---
title: "UI_P009: Single-Line UI Elements Principle"
subtitle: "Interface elements, especially navigation and control components, should present their content (icons, text, and other indicators) on a single line with consistent height whenever possible to maximize readability, maintain visual consistency, and optimize screen real estate"
chapter: "CH04"
category: "principle"
number: "UI_P009"
date-created: "2025-04-09"
date-modified: "2025-08-17"
author: "Claude"
type: "principle"
law: "UI Components Law"
article: 
  number: 9
  title: "Single-Line UI Elements Principle"
related_to:
  - "MP067: UI Separation Meta Principle"
  - "UI_P005: CSS Over Conditional Controls"
  - "DEV_P014: Performance Optimization"
  - "UI_R002: Shiny Module ID Handling"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article 9: Single-Line UI Elements Principle {#overview}

**UI Components Law Article 9 (Single-Line UI Elements Principle)**

Interface elements, especially navigation and control components, should present their content (icons, text, and other indicators) on a single line with consistent height whenever possible to maximize readability, maintain visual consistency, and optimize screen real estate.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Principle
Create cleaner, more scannable user interfaces by maintaining single-line layouts with consistent heights for interface elements, optimizing vertical space usage, and improving overall visual consistency.
:::

# Section 1: Implementation Rationale {#implementation-rationale}

## Subsection 1: Single-Line Layout Advantages {#single-line-advantages}

**Rationale for Single-Line Elements:**

1. **Readability**: Single-line elements create a cleaner, more scannable interface that users can quickly parse
2. **Space Efficiency**: Horizontal layout of UI elements preserves vertical space, which is often more constrained in web applications, especially on mobile devices
3. **Information Density**: Single-line presentation allows for higher information density without sacrificing clarity
4. **Visual Consistency**: Maintaining a consistent layout with aligned elements at uniform heights improves visual hierarchy and creates a more professional appearance
5. **Cognitive Processing**: Related elements placed on the same line with consistent height are perceived as belonging together, improving intuitive understanding of the interface
6. **Horizontal Alignment**: Elements with consistent height create a clear horizontal alignment that improves scannability and visual organization

**Layout Comparison:**

| Layout Type | Vertical Space Usage | Scannability | Visual Consistency | Responsive Adaptation |
|-------------|---------------------|--------------|-------------------|---------------------|
| Single-line | ✅ Minimized | ✅ Excellent | ✅ Highly Consistent | ⚠️ Needs Breakpoints |
| Multi-line | ❌ Wasteful | ❌ Poor | ❌ Inconsistent | ✅ Natural Wrapping |

# Section 2: Implementation Guidelines {#implementation-guidelines}

## Subsection 1: Navigation Elements {#navigation-elements}

**Navigation Element Implementation Standards:**

- Place icons and their corresponding labels on the same line
- Use flexbox with `flex-direction: row` and `align-items: center` for proper alignment
- Apply `white-space: nowrap` to prevent awkward text wrapping
- Use appropriate spacing between the icon and text (typically 5-8px)
- Set a consistent height for all navigation elements (typically 40px)
- Apply the same line-height as the element height to ensure vertical centering

**Implementation Example:**

```css
/* Proper single-line navbar elements with consistent height */
.navbar .nav-item .nav-link {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: row;
  align-items: center;
  white-space: nowrap;
  height: 40px;          /* Consistent height */
  min-height: 40px;
  line-height: 40px;     /* Match height for text alignment */
  padding-top: 0;        /* Remove default padding */
  padding-bottom: 0;
  vertical-align: middle;
}

.navbar .nav-item .nav-link i {
  margin-right: 5px;
  display: inline-flex;
  line-height: inherit;
  vertical-align: middle;
}
```

## Subsection 2: Form Elements {#form-elements}

**Form Element Layout Standards:**

- Align labels and inputs horizontally when screen space permits
- For longer forms, consider using grid layouts to maintain alignment
- Use consistent spacing across all form elements

**Implementation Example:**

```r
# Good implementation - Single-line form element
fluidRow(
  column(3, 
    tags$label("Customer Name:", style = "line-height: 34px;")
  ),
  column(9,
    textInput(ns("customer_name"), NULL, placeholder = "Enter customer name")
  )
)

# Avoid - Multi-line form element
div(
  tags$label("Customer Name:"),
  br(),  # Avoid forced line breaks
  textInput(ns("customer_name"), NULL)
)
```

# Section 3: Component Type Implementation {#component-type-implementation}

## Subsection 1: Button Groups {#button-groups}

**Button Group Standards:**

- Keep buttons in a single row when possible
- For responsive designs, define controlled breakpoints for wrapping
- Maintain consistent height across button groups

**Implementation Example:**

```css
/* Button with icon and text on same line */
.btn-with-icon {
  display: inline-flex;
  align-items: center;
  height: 38px;          /* Standard button height */
}

.btn-with-icon i {
  margin-right: 6px;
}

/* Button group container */
.btn-group-horizontal {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 8px;
  flex-wrap: nowrap;     /* Prevent wrapping */
}
```

## Subsection 2: Data Tables and Lists {#data-tables-lists}

**Table and List Standards:**

- Ensure column headers and data align properly
- Use ellipsis (...) for text truncation rather than allowing wrapping
- Consider horizontal scrolling for tables with many columns

**Implementation Example:**

```css
/* Table column text truncation */
.table td {
  white-space: nowrap;
  overflow: hidden;
  text-overflow: ellipsis;
  max-width: 200px;      /* Set maximum width */
}

/* List item single line */
.list-item {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  height: 48px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #e0e0e0;
}
```

## Subsection 3: Status Indicators {#status-indicators}

**Status Indicator Standards:**

- Place status icons/badges on the same line as their descriptive text
- Use color and shape effectively to minimize required text
- Maintain consistent positioning across similar elements

**Implementation Example:**

```r
# Status indicator with text on same line
div(
  class = "status-indicator",
  style = "display: flex; align-items: center; height: 32px;",
  icon("circle", class = "text-success mr-2"),
  span("Online", style = "line-height: 32px;")
)
```

# Section 4: Shiny/bs4Dash Implementation {#shiny-bs4dash-implementation}

## Subsection 1: Navbar Tab Implementation {#navbar-tab-implementation}

**bs4Dash Navbar Tab Patterns:**

```r
# Good implementation - Single-line navbar tab with HTML for consistent height
bs4Dash::navbarTab(
  text = HTML(paste0(
    '<span style="display:flex; align-items:center; height:40px; line-height:40px;">',
    '<i class="fa fa-chart-line mr-1" style="display:inline-flex; margin-right:5px;"></i>',
    '<span>Analysis</span>',
    '</span>'
  )),
  tabName = "analysis_tab"
)

# Standard implementation - Letting the system handle layout
bs4Dash::navbarTab(
  text = "Analysis", 
  icon = icon("chart-line"),
  tabName = "analysis_tab"
)

# Poor implementation - Breaking elements across multiple lines
div(
  icon("chart-line"),
  br(),
  "Analysis"
)
```

## Subsection 2: Sidebar Menu Implementation {#sidebar-menu-implementation}

**Sidebar Menu Patterns:**

```r
# Sidebar menu item with consistent height
bs4Dash::menuItem(
  text = "Dashboard",
  icon = icon("dashboard"),
  tabName = "dashboard",
  # Ensure single-line presentation
  badgeLabel = "New",
  badgeColor = "success"
)

# Custom sidebar item with single-line layout
div(
  class = "sidebar-item",
  style = "display: flex; align-items: center; height: 44px; padding: 0 20px;",
  icon("users", style = "margin-right: 10px;"),
  span("User Management"),
  span(
    class = "badge badge-primary ml-auto",
    "12"
  )
)
```

# Section 5: JavaScript Reinforcement {#javascript-reinforcement}

## Subsection 1: Dynamic Layout Fixes {#dynamic-layout-fixes}

**JavaScript Layout Reinforcement:**

```js
// Fix for navbar elements to ensure consistent height
$(document).ready(function() {
  function fixNavbarLayout() {
    // Standard height for all navbar elements
    const standardHeight = '40px';
    
    // Apply consistent height and layout to navbar items
    $('.navbar .nav-link, .navbarTab')
      .css('display', 'flex')
      .css('align-items', 'center')
      .css('height', standardHeight)
      .css('line-height', standardHeight);
  }
  
  // Run initially and after a delay for dynamic content
  fixNavbarLayout();
  setTimeout(fixNavbarLayout, 1000);
});
```

## Subsection 2: Responsive Breakpoint Handling {#responsive-breakpoint-handling}

**Responsive Adjustments:**

```js
// Responsive single-line to multi-line transitions
function handleResponsiveLayout() {
  const screenWidth = $(window).width();
  const breakpoint = 768; // Tablet breakpoint
  
  if (screenWidth < breakpoint) {
    // Mobile: Allow wrapping
    $('.nav-horizontal').css('flex-wrap', 'wrap');
    $('.btn-group-horizontal').css('flex-direction', 'column');
  } else {
    // Desktop: Force single line
    $('.nav-horizontal').css('flex-wrap', 'nowrap');
    $('.btn-group-horizontal').css('flex-direction', 'row');
  }
}

$(window).resize(handleResponsiveLayout);
handleResponsiveLayout(); // Initial call
```

# Section 6: Complete Implementation Example {#complete-implementation-example}

## Subsection 1: Complete Navbar Implementation {#complete-navbar-implementation}

**Comprehensive Navbar Example:**

```r
# UI function with single-line navigation
navbarUI <- function(id) {
  ns <- NS(id)
  
  tagList(
    # Custom CSS to ensure single-line layout
    tags$style(HTML(paste0(
      "#", ns("navbar"), " .nav-link {",
      "  display: flex;",
      "  align-items: center;",
      "  height: 40px;",
      "  line-height: 40px;",
      "  white-space: nowrap;",
      "}",
      "#", ns("navbar"), " .nav-link i {",
      "  margin-right: 5px;",
      "}"
    ))),
    
    # Navbar structure
    bs4Dash::bs4DashNavbar(
      id = ns("navbar"),
      skin = "light",
      status = "primary",
      
      # Left menu items
      leftUI = tagList(
        bs4Dash::navbarTab(
          text = "Dashboard",
          icon = icon("dashboard"),
          tabName = "dashboard"
        ),
        bs4Dash::navbarTab(
          text = "Reports",
          icon = icon("file-alt"),
          tabName = "reports"
        )
      ),
      
      # Right menu items
      rightUI = tagList(
        bs4Dash::navbarTab(
          text = HTML(paste0(
            '<span style="display:flex; align-items:center;">',
            '<i class="fa fa-user mr-1"></i>',
            '<span>Profile</span>',
            '</span>'
          )),
          tabName = "profile"
        )
      )
    )
  )
}
```

## Subsection 2: Complete CSS Solution {#complete-css-solution}

**Comprehensive CSS Implementation:**

```css
/* Complete CSS solution for single-line UI elements */

/* Base single-line class */
.single-line {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  white-space: nowrap;
  overflow: hidden;
}

/* Navigation elements */
.nav-single-line {
  height: 40px;
  line-height: 40px;
  padding: 0 15px;
}

/* Form elements */
.form-single-line {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 120px 1fr;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  margin-bottom: 10px;
}

/* Button groups */
.btn-group-single-line {
  display: inline-flex;
  gap: 8px;
  align-items: center;
}

/* Status indicators */
.status-single-line {
  display: inline-flex;
  align-items: center;
  height: 24px;
  padding: 0 8px;
  border-radius: 12px;
  background: #f0f0f0;
}

/* Responsive adjustments */
@media (max-width: 768px) {
  .single-line-responsive {
    flex-wrap: wrap;
  }
  
  .form-single-line {
    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  }
}
```

# Section 7: Principle Relationships {#principle-relationships}

## Subsection 1: Extending Relationships {#extending-relationships}

This principle:

1. **Implements MP067 (UI Separation)** by separating UI layout concerns from logic
2. **Complements UI_P005 (CSS Over Conditional Controls)** by using CSS rather than programmatic logic to control layout
3. **Supports DEV_P014 (Performance Optimization)** by optimizing rendering performance through reduced reflows and repaints
4. **Enhances UI_R002 (Shiny Module ID Handling)** by ensuring consistent UI behavior across modules
5. **Improves Usability** by enhancing user experience through consistent visual presentation

---

*Based on: UI Components Law Article 9*  
*Related to: [MP067 UI Separation](../../CH00_fundamental_principles/meta_principles/MP067_ui_separation.qmd), [UI_P005 CSS Over Conditional Controls](UI_P005_css_over_conditional_controls.qmd)*
